reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless Chrome real-inference verification (fresh + cached passes) + desktop/mobile screenshots + responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Real headless run (WASM, no-GPU): fresh profile → Download (~420 MB) → real output. On the cats image with multilingual labels SigLIP 2 ranked 'two cats' 0.57% (cos 0.103), '二匹の猫' 0.21% (cos 0.094), 'deux chats' 0.06% (cos 0.083) all above 'a dog' 0.02% and other scenes — the three-language cluster of 'two cats' at the top is the multilingual story working live. Console errors: 0.
rubric.notes: onnx-community/siglip2-base-patch16-224-ONNX declares model_type 'siglip', so it loads on the existing zero-shot-image-classification pipeline in transformers.js 3.7.5 — no version pin. Inference in models/siglip2-zero-shot/worker.js (Web Worker).

rubric.evidence: Expected shape confirmed: per-label independent sigmoid probabilities from logits_per_image (not a softmax that sums to 1), plus image/text cosines and a softmax column shown only for contrast. Verified live: image_embeds [1,768], text_embeds [N,768], per-label logit → sigmoid, correct ranking on two sample images.
rubric.notes: SigLIP 2 base (q8) is conservative — absolute sigmoids run under ~1% — but the logit separation between match and non-match is large and clean; the demo presents this honestly (relative bar length, true % number, logits in 'see inside').

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance shows the exact source id onnx-community/siglip2-base-patch16-224-ONNX linked to its card (and the google/siglip2 origin), task zero-shot-image-classification, backend WebAssembly, quantisation q8, download ~420 MB, license Apache-2.0, and the Gemma multilingual tokenizer + max_length 64 detail. Off the main thread (worker.js).
rubric.notes: Honest disclosure of the 'new vs SigLIP 1' delta (multilingual Gemma tokenizer, 2025 training recipe) and the model_type='siglip' compatibility fact — no invented API.

rubric.evidence: Two-pass headless test on a persistent profile. PASS 1 (fresh): loader 'download-required' + Download (~420 MB) button — no silent transfer. PASS 2 (cached reload): loader 'ready' immediately, NO Download button, real output rendered — auto-init from cache works per the shared createModelLoader contract.
rubric.notes: shared-auto-init-loader = pass; per-model clear-cache control present; ~420 MB is honestly disclosed so the visitor knows what they're committing to.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live announces load/scoring/done/error; a .err status surfaces scoring failures. Verified download-required, ready, and 'Done.' states live; the shared loader provides retry.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and eviction paths handled by the shared loader but not separately driven this pass.

rubric.evidence: Real controls: drag/drop + file picker, three sample thumbnails (keyboard-activatable), a labels textarea, two preset chips, a hypothesis-template <select>, a fine-grained match-threshold range (re-renders live), and Score/Reset buttons. Verified Score triggers a real run and the threshold re-highlights matches without re-inferring.
rubric.notes: Threshold slider maps 1–200 → 0.01–2.00% to suit SigLIP 2's low scores. A driven keyboard/touch traversal at 360px is the remaining check before flipping mobile to ok.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile shows the honest absent-model state and empty bars; scores appear only after a real on-device run. The low absolute sigmoids are the model's REAL output (verified), presented honestly rather than rescaled to look confident.
rubric.notes: Bar length is explicitly labelled as relative-to-top; the number is the true independent probability.

rubric.evidence: buttons-have-names and images-have-alt hold (sample thumbnails + preview have descriptive alt). Bars use role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label; the dropzone is a role=button with keyboard handlers; status region is role=status aria-live.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline; a full SR pass over the results table would be a nice add.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check after an in-place fix: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth<=innerWidth, zero clipped controls, console+network clean. Initial mobile scan flagged 2 clipped long model-id links in the At-a-glance table; fixed guidance-first by removing white-space:nowrap and adding overflow-wrap:anywhere so the ids wrap. Mobile screenshot confirms legible reflow.
rubric.notes: Desktop flipped to ok (full driven matrix pass); mobile kept needs-review (automated-clean + screenshot) pending a driven touch pass.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a Web Worker (off main thread); the threshold slider re-renders from the cached last result without re-inferring, so dragging it stays smooth. Verified the page stays responsive during the ~420 MB load.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement while scoring a large uploaded image would confirm the encode path stays off the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing (flex: 1 1 <basis>, min-inline-size:0), and let long unbreakable tokens wrap (overflow-wrap:anywhere) so nothing forces horizontal page overflow; wide tables scroll inside overflow-x:auto.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the image/label columns use flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0; tables sit in .table-scroll; removed a white-space:nowrap that made long HF-id links overflow at 360px and added overflow-wrap:anywhere. Re-verified mobile clean.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check mobile fixed from 2 clipped → 0 clipped; reports/responsive/screens/siglip2-zero-shot/mobile.png

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread; avoid re-computing on every slider tick.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — SigLIP 2 inference runs in models/siglip2-zero-shot/worker.js; the threshold slider re-renders from the cached result rather than re-inferring.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: inference-off-main-thread source assertion; slider re-render path

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a live region; give every control an accessible name; expose score bars to assistive tech.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live; role=meter bars with aria-valuenow/label; keyboard-operable dropzone + thumbnails.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css (invariant 6).

openQuestions: Drive a full touch-interaction pass at 360×740 (drag/drop, template select, threshold slider tap target) before flipping mobile support to ok.

SigLIP 2 base q8 scores are ~40× lower in absolute terms than SigLIP v1's q8 — is this an int8-quantisation calibration effect on the large Gemma text tower, and would a fp16 text tower restore the magnitude without an unreasonable download?
The French homograph 'chat' (cat vs online-chat) can dip below dog words — worth surfacing as a teachable edge in the cross-lingual demo?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for siglip2-zero-shot: drive drop/upload + threshold + template select on mobile with touch, verify tap targets/focus, then flip mobile support to ok.

Add a 'multilingual search index' multi-model demo: embed a small image set with SigLIP 2, then query the index with a text label in any language and rank the gallery — showcasing cross-lingual retrieval, not just single-image scoring.

summary: siglip2-zero-shot is a strong, honest demo of Google's SigLIP 2 (2025), distinct from the built SigLIP v1 by its multilingual Gemma tokenizer: real zero-shot-image-classification via onnx-community/siglip2-base-patch16-224-ONNX (loads as model_type 'siglip' on transformers.js 3.7.5, no version pin) in a Web Worker, verified end-to-end in headless Chrome — fresh Download → correct multilingual output ('two cats'/'二匹の猫'/'deux chats' cluster above 'a dog'), cached reload → auto-init no button, console clean both passes. It keeps SigLIP's independent-sigmoid read-out and the sigmoid-vs-softmax 'see inside', and presents SigLIP 2's conservative absolute scores honestly (relative bar length, true % number, logits as the reliable signal). A mobile overflow (long HF-id links) was fixed in place guidance-first. Desktop support flipped to ok; mobile kept needs-review pending a driven touch pass. Revision 1.
